description: Dependabot/Snyk/npm audit or equivalent runs on every PR and on a schedule not just manually when someone remembers
why_it_matters: Known-vulnerable dependencies are the most common real-world breach vector and scanning is nearly free to automate

check: Lockfiles committed and enforced in CI
description: package-lock.json/pnpm-lock.yaml/poetry.lock committed and CI fails on lockfile drift (npm ci not npm install)
why_it_matters: Without a locked and enforced lockfile a compromised transitive dependency can be silently pulled in on any install

description: A lightweight review step (maintainer count, download trend, last publish date, install scripts) before adding a new package not just before major ones
why_it_matters: Most supply-chain compromises land through a small unreviewed package not a headline framework

check: Package installs pinned to exact versions for security-sensitive dependencies
description: Auth crypto and payment-adjacent packages pinned to exact versions rather than caret ranges so an update requires a deliberate action
why_it_matters: Caret ranges auto-accept patch updates which is how several real npm supply-chain attacks propagated silently

check: Provenance or signature verification used where available
description: npm provenance attestations Sigstore or equivalent checked for critical dependencies where the ecosystem supports it
why_it_matters: Verifies a package actually came from its claimed source and build rather than a hijacked publish

check: CI secrets scoped to the minimum required per job
description: CI/CD pipeline credentials scoped per job/environment rather than one broad token available to every workflow step
why_it_matters: A compromised or malicious PR-triggered workflow step should not have access to unrelated production secrets

check: Third-party GitHub Actions pinned to a commit SHA not a mutable tag
description: Actions from the marketplace pinned to a full commit SHA (or first-party actions only) rather than a floating tag like @v3
why_it_matters: A tag can be silently repointed to malicious code by the action's own maintainer account being compromised

description: Deployed artifacts traced back to a specific reviewed commit and CI run rather than deployed from an untracked local build
why_it_matters: Prevents a compromised local machine or unreviewed change from reaching production outside the normal pipeline

check: Software Bill of Materials generated for production builds
description: An SBOM (CycloneDX/SPDX) generated per release so the full dependency tree is known without re-deriving it during an incident
why_it_matters: When a dependency is disclosed vulnerable an SBOM answers am I affected in minutes instead of hours

check: Dependency update and patch process has a defined SLA
description: A documented target (e.g. Critical CVEs patched within 72 hours) for acting on a disclosed dependency vulnerability not an ad hoc response
why_it_matters: Scanning without an SLA to act on findings just produces an ignored backlog of known vulnerabilities
